---
title: "Resources"
subtitle: "Books, courses, papers, and tools I actually use — not a wish list."
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

A curated collection of the material I go back to. Not an exhaustive
list — just what has been genuinely useful for my work in causal
inference, pricing, and forecasting.

If something on this page led you somewhere interesting, let me know.

---

## Books

### Causal Inference

- **Angrist & Pischke** — *Mostly Harmless Econometrics* (2009).
  The classic. Read chapters 3 (regression) and 5 (DiD) at least twice.
- **Cunningham** — *Causal Inference: The Mixtape* (2021).
  Free online: <https://mixtape.scunning.com/>. The most practitioner-friendly
  introduction to identification strategies.
- **Hernán & Robins** — *Causal Inference: What If* (2020).
  Free online: <https://www.hsph.harvard.edu/miguel-hernan/causal-inference-book/>.
  The potential outcomes framework, done rigorously.
- **Pearl, Glymour & Jewell** — *Causal Inference in Statistics: A Primer* (2016).
  The graphical / DAG side of causal inference.
- **Facure** — *Causal Inference for the Brave and True* (2022).
  Free online: <https://matheusfacure.github.io/python-causality-handbook/>.
  Python-first, notebook-style walkthroughs.

### Pricing, Demand & Revenue Management

- **Phillips** — *Pricing and Revenue Optimization* (2021, 2nd ed.).
  The book on the subject.
- **Talluri & van Ryzin** — *The Theory and Practice of Revenue Management* (2004).
  Deeper on the airline/hotel revenue management side.
- **Ariely** — *Predictably Irrational* (2008).
  Behavioural side of pricing. Short, useful.

### Forecasting

- **Hyndman & Athanasopoulos** — *Forecasting: Principles and Practice* (2021, 3rd ed.).
  Free online: <https://otexts.com/fpp3/>. The reference.
- **Petropoulos et al.** — *Forecasting: theory and practice* (2022).
  A 300-page survey paper. Free: <https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.03854>.

### Experimentation

- **Kohavi, Tang & Xu** — *Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments* (2020).
  Written by people who run experiments at scale (Microsoft, Airbnb, LinkedIn).
  If you only read one book on A/B testing, read this one.
- **Gerber & Green** — *Field Experiments: Design, Analysis, and Interpretation* (2012).
  The academic counterpart. Strong on design.

### Bayesian Methods

- **McElreath** — *Statistical Rethinking* (2020, 2nd ed.).
  The book that makes Bayesian inference click. Companion lectures on YouTube.
- **Gelman et al.** — *Bayesian Data Analysis* (2013, 3rd ed.).
  Free online: <https://sites.stat.columbia.edu/gelman/book/>. The reference.

---

## Courses

- **MIT OCW — 14.32 Econometrics** (undergraduate).  
<https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-32-econometrics-spring-2007/>
  
- **MIT OCW — 14.382 Econometrics** (graduate).  
<https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-382-econometrics-spring-2017/>
  
- **MIT OCW — 14.310x Data Analysis for Social Scientists** (Duflo & Ellison).  
<https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-310x-data-analysis-for-social-scientists-spring-2023/>  
  Probability, statistics, regression, and applied causal inference framed
around policy questions. Great pairing with *Mostly Harmless Econometrics* —
the textbook gives you the theory, this course gives you the applied intuition.

- **MIT OCW — 14.750x Political Economy and Economic Development** (Duflo & Banerjee).  
<https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-750-political-economy-and-economic-development-spring-2018/>  
  Advanced applied causal inference in development economics, from the
Nobel-winning J-PAL team.

- **The Effect — Video Series (Nick Huntington-Klein)**.  
<https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLwJRxp3blEvZyQBTTOMFRP_TDaSdly3gU>  
  Full companion video series to the book *The Effect*. 69+ episodes on
research design and causal inference, from DAGs to IV to synthetic control.
Book (free): <https://theeffectbook.net/>.

- **The Effect — Video Series (Nick Huntington-Klein)**.
  <https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLwJRxp3blEvZyQBTTOMFRP_TDaSdly3gU>
  Full companion video series to the book *The Effect*. 69+ episodes on
  research design and causal inference, from DAGs to IV to synthetic control.

- **Statistical Rethinking Lectures — McElreath** (YouTube).  
<https://github.com/rmcelreath/stat_rethinking_2024>  
  The best Bayesian course, free.

- **Causal Inference Bootcamp — Duke / Kyle Butts**.  
<https://kylebutts.com/econ-teaching.html>



## Blogs & Practitioner Sites

### Econometrics & Causal Inference

- **Chris Bilder** — <https://www.chrisbilder.com/>.
  Categorical data and applied stats, with R code you can actually run.
- **Andrew Gelman** — <https://statmodeling.stat.columbia.edu/>.
  Bayesian statistics, methodology critique, occasional politics.
- **Marc Bellemare** — <https://marcfbellemare.com/wordpress/>.
  Applied econometrics, IV, and academic career advice.
- **Ryan Cornelius (Very Sensible)** — <https://verysensible.com/>.
  Causal inference for practitioners.
- **Andrew Heiss** — <https://www.andrewheiss.com/blog/>.
  Bayesian econometrics with R walkthroughs.
- **Nick Huntington-Klein** — <https://nickchk.com/>.
  Econometrics teaching, R packages, and thoughtful writing.

### Experimentation & Product Data Science

- **Netflix Tech Blog — Experimentation** —
  <https://netflixtechblog.com/tagged/experimentation>.
- **Airbnb Data Science** — <https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/ai-ml>.
- **Uber Engineering — Experimentation** —
  <https://www.uber.com/en-PT/blog/engineering/>.
- **Booking.com — Data & Experimentation** —
  <https://booking.ai/>.
- **Spotify R&D** — <https://research.atspotify.com/>.

### Pricing, MMM & Marketing Science

- **PyMC Labs Blog** — <https://www.pymc-labs.com/blog-posts/>.
  Bayesian MMM, marketing measurement, PyMC-Marketing tutorials.
- **Google Ads Attribution & Incrementality**
  (search "Google Ads Data Hub" and "geo experiments" documentation).

### Forecasting

- **Rob Hyndman's Blog** — <https://robjhyndman.com/hyndsight/>.
  From the co-author of *Forecasting: Principles and Practice*.
- **Nixtla Blog** — <https://nixtla.github.io/nixtla/>.
  Python-first forecasting stack (StatsForecast, MLForecast, NeuralForecast).

### General

- **R-bloggers** — <https://www.r-bloggers.com/>.
  Aggregator of R content. Set up an RSS feed on causal / forecasting tags.

---

## Papers I keep coming back to

### DiD / Staggered Adoption

- Callaway & Sant'Anna (2021). *Difference-in-differences with multiple time periods*. Journal of Econometrics. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001>
- Goodman-Bacon (2021). *Difference-in-differences with variation in treatment timing*. Journal of Econometrics.
- Sun & Abraham (2021). *Estimating dynamic treatment effects in event studies with heterogeneous treatment effects*. Journal of Econometrics.
- Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski & Poe (2023). *What's trending in difference-in-differences?* Journal of Econometrics.

### Synthetic Control

- Abadie, Diamond & Hainmueller (2010). *Synthetic control methods for comparative case studies*. JASA.
- Abadie (2021). *Using synthetic controls: feasibility, data requirements, and methodological aspects*. JEL.

### Regression Discontinuity

- Cattaneo, Idrobo & Titiunik (2019). *A practical introduction to regression discontinuity designs*. Cambridge.

### DML & Causal Forests

- Chernozhukov et al. (2018). *Double/debiased machine learning*. Econometrics Journal.
- Wager & Athey (2018). *Estimation and inference of heterogeneous treatment effects using random forests*. JASA.

### Experimentation

- Deng, Xu, Kohavi & Walker (2013). *Improving the sensitivity of online controlled experiments by utilizing pre-experiment data* — the CUPED paper.
- Bojinov, Simchi-Levi & Zhao (2023). *Design and analysis of switchback experiments*. Management Science.

### Marketing Mix Modeling

- Jin, Wang, Sun, Chan & Koehler (2017). *Bayesian methods for media mix modeling with carryover and shape effects*. Google.

---

## R Packages (that I actually use)

### Causal Inference

- **`fixest`** — <https://lrberge.github.io/fixest/>.
  Fast fixed-effects regression. My default for DiD and panel data.
- **`did`** — <https://bcallaway11.github.io/did/>.
  Callaway & Sant'Anna staggered-adoption DiD.
- **`Synth`** — synthetic control.
- **`rdrobust`** — Cattaneo et al. RDD implementation.
- **`grf`** — <https://grf-labs.github.io/grf/>.
  Generalized random forests (causal forests, DML).
- **`DoubleML`** — <https://docs.doubleml.org/>.
  Chernozhukov et al. DML in R.
- **`marginaleffects`** — <https://marginaleffects.com/>.
  Interpretation of anything, from OLS to Bayesian.
- **`adjustedCurves`** — G-computation for survival.

### Forecasting

- **`fable`** and **`fabletools`** — <https://fable.tidyverts.org/>.
  Hyndman's tidyverse forecasting stack.
- **`forecast`** — the older classic.
- **`prophet`** — <https://facebook.github.io/prophet/>.

### Bayesian

- **`brms`** — <https://paul-buerkner.github.io/brms/>.
  Bayesian regression, Stan under the hood, formula syntax.
- **`rstanarm`** — Bayesian versions of standard R models.
- **`CausalImpact`** — Google's BSTS for causal inference.

### General

- **`tidyverse`** — `dplyr`, `ggplot2`, `tidyr`, `purrr`.
- **`patchwork`** — combining ggplot2 figures.
- **`scales`** — proper axis formatting.
- **`gt`** and **`kableExtra`** — tables that don't embarrass you.

---

## Python

- **`statsmodels`** — the basics: OLS, panel, GLM.
- **`linearmodels`** — panel data, IV, and system estimators.
- **`econml`** — <https://econml.azurewebsites.net/>.
  Microsoft's DML / meta-learners / policy learning.
- **`pymc`** — <https://www.pymc.io/>.
  Probabilistic programming, general-purpose Bayesian modelling.
- **`pymc-marketing`** — <https://www.pymc-marketing.io/>.
  Bayesian MMM out of the box: adstock, saturation, budget optimisation.
- **`causalinference`** — implements matching and other estimators.
- **`dowhy`** — <https://www.pywhy.org/dowhy/>.
  Causal inference with a graphical model layer.
- **`nixtla` stack** — `statsforecast`, `mlforecast`, `neuralforecast`.

---

## Communities

- **Causal Inference Slack** (invite through the Mixtape site).
- **R for Data Science online community** — <https://rfordatasci.com/>.
- **Cross Validated** (stats.stackexchange.com) — still the best place to
  ask sharp methodological questions.

---

*If a link here is broken or you have a strong recommendation to add,
let me know via [email](mailto:joaoviei7a@gmail.com) or LinkedIn.*
